title: MAN Warns of Economic Disruption from Single-Use Plastics Ban, Calls for Collaborative Approach - FinancialEDGE
description: The Manufacturers Association of Nigeria (MAN) has expressed concerns over the potential economic implications of the proposed nationwide ban on single-use plastics. html:
The Manufacturers Association of Nigeria (MAN) has expressed concerns over the potential economic implications of the proposed nationwide ban on single-use plastics.

In a recent report which was made available to Financial Edge, MAN highlighted the significant impact on businesses across various sectors, including manufacturing, packaging, and consumer goods.

The association warned that the ban could lead to substantial investments in research and development, supply chain disruptions, and potential job losses.

MAN emphasized the need for a collaborative approach between government, stakeholders, and businesses to mitigate these disruptions and ensure a smoother transition to a circular economy.

MAN proposed a phased implementation of the ban, adequate government support, and incentives to offset the costs of adopting alternative materials.

The association also suggested establishing a dedicated fund to support Research and Development into sustainable packaging solutions.

By working collaboratively, MAN aims to minimize business closures and job losses while promoting a circular economy.

The association is committed to finding sustainable solutions that balance environmental concerns with economic growth.
